etymology.interpretation: The character 丐 was an alteration of 匄(匄). The original meaning of 匄(匄) is unknown, with suggestions it might have meant 'to damage'. Over time, 辛(辛) (xīn, 'bitter') was mistakenly altered, leading to the present form 丐.

examples.chinese: 他曾经是一个丐幫的成员(他曾經是一個丐幫的成員)。
examples.pinyin: Tā céngjīng shì yīgè gàibāng de chéngyuán.
examples.english: He was once a member of the Beggar Clan.

examples.chinese: 街上有个老丐(街上有個老丐)，他总是微笑着(他總是微笑著)。
examples.pinyin: Jiē shàng yǒu gè lǎo gài, tā zǒng shì wéixiào zhe.
examples.english: There is an old beggar on the street who always smiles.

examples.chinese: 那名丐衣衫褴褛(那名丐衣衫襤褸)，但眼神充满智慧(但眼神充滿智慧)。
examples.pinyin: Nà míng gài yīshān lánlǚ, dàn yǎnshén chōngmǎn zhìhuì.
examples.english: The beggar was ragged, but his eyes were full of wisdom.
